title: Nightly Salesforce import truncates international phone country codes (3+ digit codes)
problem: The nightly batch import that syncs customer data from Salesforce into TaskFlow is corrupting international phone numbers by truncating their country codes. Numbers with longer country codes (3-4 digits) are affected while shorter codes (1-2 digits like US +1 and UK +44) appear unaffected. Approximately 50-100 of ~2,000 customer records are impacted, and the corruption is persisted to storage — wrong numbers appear in the UI, exports, and presumably the database.
root_cause_hypothesis: The phone number parsing/normalization logic in the Salesforce-to-TaskFlow import pipeline is truncating country codes to a fixed length (likely 2 digits) or misidentifying country code boundaries. Ireland's +353 becomes +35, Antigua's +1268 becomes +12. This is consistent with a parser that assumes all country codes are 1-2 digits, or a recent change to a phone number parsing library that introduced this behavior. A code deploy or dependency update ~2 weeks ago likely introduced the regression.
reproduction_steps: Identify a customer record in Salesforce with a 3+ digit country code (e.g., Ireland +353, India +91, Antigua +1268)
Ensure the phone number is correctly formatted in Salesforce
Wait for or manually trigger the nightly batch import sync
Check the resulting phone number in TaskFlow — expect the country code to be truncated
environment: Production. Nightly batch import from Salesforce CRM to TaskFlow. Regression appeared approximately 2 weeks ago (late March 2026). Affects international numbers with 3+ digit country codes; US (+1) and UK (+44) numbers unaffected.
impact: Support team cannot reach ~50-100 international customers by phone. Data integrity of customer contact records is compromised. Each nightly sync overwrites previously correct numbers with corrupted ones, so the problem compounds as more records are re-synced.
recommended_fix: 1. Check deploy/change history for the import pipeline from ~2 weeks ago — look for changes to phone number normalization, parsing libraries (e.g., libphonenumber), or the Salesforce API integration. 2. Inspect the import code's country code parsing logic for assumptions about country code length. 3. Fix the parser to correctly handle variable-length country codes (1-3 digits per ITU-T E.164). 4. Run a one-time remediation sync to re-import corrected numbers from Salesforce for all affected records. 5. Consider adding a validation check that flags phone numbers whose parsed country code doesn't match a known valid code.
proposed_test_case: Create test cases for the phone number import/normalization function using numbers with 1-digit (+1 US), 2-digit (+44 UK), and 3-digit (+353 Ireland, +268 Antigua landline) country codes. Assert that the full country code and subscriber number are preserved after normalization. Include edge cases like +1268 (NANP overlay with Antigua) to verify correct code boundary detection.
information_gaps: Exact date of the first corrupted sync (engineering can check import logs)
Whether a specific code deploy or dependency update coincides with the timeline
Whether records that were not re-synced in the last 2 weeks still have correct numbers
The specific phone number library or normalization function used in the import pipeline
